ccel.title: NPNF1-02. St. Augustine's City of God and Christian Doctrine

title: Whether Rome Ought to Have Been Entrusted to the Trojan Gods.

source.title: NPNF1-02. St. Augustine's City of God and Christian Doctrine

verses.text: Where, then, was the wisdom of entrusting Rome to the Trojan gods, who had demonstrated their weakness in the loss of Troy?
Will some one say that, when Fimbria stormed Troy, the gods were already resident in Rome?
How, then, did the image of Minerva remain standing?
Besides, if they were at Rome when Fimbria destroyed Troy, perhaps they were at Troy when Rome itself was taken and set on fire by the Gauls.

But as they are very acute in hearing, and very swift in their movements, they came quickly at the cackling of the goose to defend at least the Capitol, though to defend the rest of the city they were too long in being warned.
